Annona nicaraguensis is a plant species in the genus Annona, characterized by its small size and fragrant white flowers. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Annona nicaraguensis include annonins, which have been shown to possess neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
